wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Project Overview section focused on Accomplish AI Desktop Agent. Explain its purpose as an open-source AI desktop automation platform that runs locally on user machines, automating file management, document creation, and browser tasks. Document the core value propositions including local-first architecture, privacy-focused design, and multi-provider AI integration. Describe the target audience, key use cases, and system requirements. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners explaining what the platform does and why it matters, and technical details for experienced developers covering the architecture and capabilities. Use terminology consistent with the codebase such as 'task execution', 'permission system', and 'MCP tools'. Provide practical examples demonstrating common automation scenarios like file organization, document drafting, and browser workflows. Document the relationship between the desktop application, daemon process, and web interface components.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive system architecture documentation for Accomplish. Describe the overall system design showing the three-tier architecture with Electron desktop application, background daemon process, and shared agent-core library. Include component dependency relationships, shared library usage, and the rationale behind this distributed architecture. Document the technology stack including Electron for desktop, React for web interface, TypeScript for type safety, and the MCP tool framework. Provide system context diagrams showing data flow between components and highlight the separation of concerns between UI, business logic, and task execution layers.

Explain the main process responsibilities including application lifecycle management, window creation, and system integration. Document the renderer process role in UI rendering and user interaction. Use terminology consistent with Electron development. Provide practical examples demonstrating process communication patterns, lifecycle hooks, and startup/shutdown procedures. Document the relationship between main process and renderer process, including security implications and best practices.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Accomplish: Desktop AI Agent Platform

## Project Introduction

Accomplish is an open-source AI coworker platform that brings intelligent task automation to desktop environments. The project serves as a comprehensive solution for AI-powered productivity, featuring a sophisticated desktop application built with Electron, a background daemon process for task execution, and a rich ecosystem of AI providers and MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools.

### Purpose Statement
Accomplish transforms how users interact with AI by providing a seamless desktop experience where AI agents can perform complex tasks, manage permissions, and integrate with various AI providers through a unified interface.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Desktop Integration**: Deliver AI capabilities directly within the user's desktop environment
- **Multi-Provider Support**: Abstract AI provider complexity behind a unified interface
- **Task Automation**: Enable sophisticated AI-driven task execution with permission management
- **Extensibility**: Provide a framework for custom MCP tools and skills
- **Cross-Platform**: Support Windows, macOS, and Linux environments

### Target Audience
- Knowledge workers seeking AI assistance
- Developers building AI-powered applications
- Teams requiring automated workflow solutions
- Organizations needing secure AI task execution

## Technical Architecture

The project follows a sophisticated microservice-like architecture with clear separation of concerns across three main components:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Electron Desktop App] --> B[Daemon Process]
    B --> C[Task Manager]
    B --> D[Storage Service]
    B --> E[Permission Service]
    B --> F[Thought Stream Service]
    
    C --> G[OpenCode Adapter]
    G --> H[AI Providers]
    H --> I[OpenAI]
    H --> J[AWS Bedrock]
    H --> K[Google Vertex]
    H --> L[Ollama]
    
    M[MCP Tools] --> N[File Permission]
    M --> O[Desktop Control]
    M --> P[Dev Browser]
    M --> Q[Start Task]
    
    R[Web Interface] --> A
    S[Agent Core Library] --> A
    S --> B
```

### Component Breakdown

**Desktop Application Layer**
- Electron-based main process handling window management and system integration
- Renderer process for user interface interactions
- IPC communication bridge between main and renderer processes

**Daemon Process Layer**
- Background service for task execution and resource management
- Socket-based RPC communication for inter-process coordination
- Persistent storage and state management

**Core Library Layer**
- Shared business logic and type definitions
- Provider abstraction layer for AI services
- MCP tool framework for extensible functionality

### Design Patterns

The architecture implements several key design patterns:

- **Factory Pattern**: Used extensively for creating service instances with consistent interfaces
- **Observer Pattern**: Event-driven communication between components
- **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable AI provider implementations
- **Command Pattern**: Task execution and permission handling
- **Singleton Pattern**: Centralized service management

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant UI as Desktop UI
    participant DA as Daemon API
    participant TM as Task Manager
    participant OA as OpenCode Adapter
    participant AP as AI Provider
    
    UI->>DA: Task Request
    DA->>TM: Execute Task
    TM->>OA: Start Task
    OA->>AP: Execute Request
    AP-->>OA: Response
    OA-->>TM: Messages
    TM-->>DA: Progress Updates
    DA-->>UI: Real-time Updates
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Desktop Application Entry**
- `apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts` - Electron main process bootstrap
- Handles window creation, IPC setup, and system integration

**Daemon Process Entry**
- `apps/daemon/src/index.ts` - Background task execution service
- Manages task lifecycle, permission handling, and inter-process communication

**Web Application Entry**
- `apps/web/src/client/main.tsx` - React-based web interface
- Provides alternative access method for task management

### Core Modules

**Agent Core Library**
- `packages/agent-core/src/index.ts` - Public API surface
- Factory functions for service creation: `createTaskManager`, `createStorage`, `createPermissionHandler`
- Shared type definitions and interfaces

**Task Management System**
- `packages/agent-core/src/internal/classes/TaskManager.ts` - Core task orchestration
- Implements queuing, concurrency control, and lifecycle management
- Handles OpenCode adapter integration and message processing

**Provider Integration**
- `packages/agent-core/src/providers/index.ts` - Unified AI provider interface
- Supports OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex, Ollama, and custom providers
- Consistent authentication and model discovery across providers

### Configuration Approach

**Workspace Configuration**
- `packages/agent-core/src/storage/repositories/workspaces.js` - Multi-workspace support
- `packages/agent-core/src/store/workspaceManager.ts` - Workspace lifecycle management

**Environment Setup**
- `packages/agent-core/src/opencode/config-generator.js` - OpenCode configuration generation
- `packages/agent-core/src/opencode/environment.js` - Runtime environment management

### External Dependencies

**Core Dependencies**
- `@accomplish_ai/agent-core` - Internal core library
- `electron` - Desktop application framework
- `better-sqlite3` - Local database storage
- `zod` - Schema validation
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` - MCP tool framework

**Development Dependencies**
- `typescript` - Type safety and modern JavaScript
- `vitest` - Testing framework
- `pnpm` - Package management with monorepo support

### Integration Points

**MCP Tool Ecosystem**
- `packages/agent-core/mcp-tools/` - Extensible tool framework
- Includes file permission handling, desktop control, and development tools
- Standardized interface for AI agent capabilities

**Authentication Flow**
- `packages/agent-core/src/opencode/auth.js` - OAuth and API key management
- `apps/desktop/src/opencode/auth-browser.ts` - Browser-based authentication
- Slack MCP OAuth integration for enhanced connectivity

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class TaskManager {
        +startTask()
        +cancelTask()
        +queueTask()
        +processQueue()
    }
    
    class OpenCodeAdapter {
        +startTask()
        +sendResponse()
        +cancelTask()
    }
    
    class ProviderInterface {
        +validateCredentials()
        +fetchModels()
        +executeRequest()
    }
    
    class StorageService {
        +getTask()
        +updateTask()
        +listTasks()
    }
    
    TaskManager --> OpenCodeAdapter
    OpenCodeAdapter --> ProviderInterface
    TaskManager --> StorageService
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Multi-Provider AI Integration**
- Unified interface for OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex, Ollama, and custom providers
- Automatic credential validation and model discovery
- Provider-specific optimization and fallback mechanisms

**Advanced Task Management**
- Concurrent task execution with configurable limits
- Sophisticated queuing and prioritization systems
- Real-time progress tracking and status updates

**Permission and Security System**
- Granular file operation permissions
- Interactive question/response permission handling
- Secure credential storage and management

**Extensible MCP Tool Framework**
- Standardized interface for AI agent capabilities
- Built-in tools for file operations, desktop control, and development
- Easy addition of custom tools and skills

### Implementation Highlights

**Task Lifecycle Management**
- `packages/agent-core/src/internal/classes/TaskManager.ts` - Comprehensive task orchestration
- Implements queuing, cancellation, and progress tracking
- Handles OpenCode adapter lifecycle and error recovery

**Provider Abstraction Layer**
- `packages/agent-core/src/providers/` - Consistent interface across AI providers
- Provider-specific validation and connection testing
- Dynamic model discovery and selection

**Desktop Integration**
- `apps/desktop/src/main/` - Electron main process implementation
- System tray integration and window management
- Native OS feature access (notifications, file system)

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Queued
    Queued --> Running: Start Task
    Running --> Completed: Success
    Running --> Failed: Error
    Running --> Cancelled: User Cancel
    Completed --> [*]
    Failed --> [*]
    Cancelled --> [*]
    
    state Running {
        [*] --> Initializing
        Initializing --> Planning: Setup Complete
        Planning --> Executing: Plan Created
        Executing --> Waiting: Permission Needed
        Waiting --> Executing: Permission Granted
        Executing --> Finalizing: Task Complete
        Finalizing --> Completed: Results Saved
    }
```

**Real-time Communication**
- WebSocket-based thought stream for AI reasoning visualization
- IPC bridges for desktop integration
- REST API endpoints for external service integration

**Cross-Platform Compatibility**
- Native OS integration (Windows, macOS, Linux)
- Platform-specific optimizations and security features
- Consistent user experience across platforms

The Accomplish project represents a mature, enterprise-grade solution for AI-powered desktop productivity, combining robust technical architecture with intuitive user experience design.

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [pnpm-workspace.yaml](pnpm-workspace.yaml)
- [packages/agent-core/package.json](packages/agent-core/package.json)
- [packages/agent-core/src/index.ts](packages/agent-core/src/index.ts)
- [apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts](apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts)
- [apps/web/src/client/main.tsx](apps/web/src/client/main.tsx)
- [apps/daemon/src/index.ts](apps/daemon/src/index.ts)
- [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md)
- [packages/agent-core/src/internal/classes/TaskManager.ts](packages/agent-core/src/internal/classes/TaskManager.ts)
- [packages/agent-core/src/factories/index.ts](packages/agent-core/src/factories/index.ts)
- [packages/agent-core/src/providers/index.ts](packages/agent-core/src/providers/index.ts)
- [packages/agent-core/mcp-tools/start-task/src/index.ts](packages/agent-core/mcp-tools/start-task/src/index.ts)

</blog>
